title: Write 'How to Add a New ML Mode' contributor guide
description:
THE #1 DX IMPROVEMENT. No documentation exists for adding a new mode to the ML module. A developer currently spends ~2 hours reverse-engineering the 3,737-line monolith.

Create docs/guides/ml-design-new-mode.md covering:
1. Prerequisites (understand the mode pattern, TypeScript unions)
2. Step 1: Create engine file (src/lib/ml-design/your-mode.ts) — template provided
3. Step 2: Export types in index.ts
4. Step 3: Add to ModuleMode union (MLDesignModule.tsx:57)
5. Step 4: Add to MODE_TABS array (line 78)
6. Step 5: Create Sidebar, Canvas, Properties, BottomPanel components
7. Step 6: Register in MLSidebar (line 983), canvas/properties/bottomPanel sections
8. Step 7: Add state variables to useMLDesignModule hook
9. Step 8: Write tests
10. Checklist before submitting PR

Include a complete annotated template for a minimal mode (e.g., 'Hello World ML Mode').
acceptanceCriteria: Guide exists at docs/guides/ml-design-new-mode.md
Covers all 10 steps with file paths and line numbers
Includes copyable template code for engine + types
Includes PR checklist with quality criteria
A new developer can add a mode in <2 hours using this guide
Guide references content style guide (MLD-127) for educational content

title: Create ML mode scaffolding script
description:
Automate new mode creation to reduce errors and save ~1 hour per mode:

```bash
pnpm scaffold:ml-mode --name reinforcement-learning --label 'Reinforcement Learning' --icon Gamepad2
```

Creates:
- src/lib/ml-design/reinforcement-learning.ts (engine boilerplate with types)
- Updates src/lib/ml-design/index.ts (adds exports)
- Prints remaining manual steps: 'Add to ModuleMode union, MODE_TABS, sidebar/canvas/properties rendering'

The manual steps can't be auto-generated because they're inline in the monolith. After MLD-028 (split the monolith), the script could create component files too.
acceptanceCriteria: Script creates engine file with boilerplate types and functions
Script updates barrel export index.ts
Script prints clear next-steps checklist
Generated code compiles without errors
Template includes JSDoc annotations and educational comments

title: Write ML Design architecture overview document
description:
No architecture documentation exists. Create docs/architecture/ml-design.md with:

1. Module overview: 6 modes, their purposes, target audience
2. Data flow diagram (from types audit): User → Hook → Engine → Result → Visualizer
3. File organization: lib/ml-design/ (engines), components/modules/MLDesignModule.tsx (UI), components/ml-design/ (ORPHANED — explain status)
4. State management: ~30 useState in useMLDesignModule hook, no Zustand store
5. Type system: layer diagram showing INPUT → ENGINE → STEP → RESULT types
6. Known issues: optimizer bug, orphaned files, monolith to be split
7. Cross-module integration: bridge system status, progress store status

This is the document a new developer reads FIRST before touching any code.
acceptanceCriteria: Architecture doc at docs/architecture/ml-design.md
Data flow diagram included (Mermaid or ASCII)
File organization map with purpose of each directory
Known issues section with task references
Document takes <15 minutes to read
Updated when major architecture changes are made

title: Add README.md to src/lib/ml-design/ explaining the library
description:
The ml-design library directory has 17 files and zero README. A developer finding this directory has no context.

Create src/lib/ml-design/README.md:
- What this library provides (pure computation functions for ML concepts)
- File index with 1-line description per file
- Which files are actively used vs orphaned
- How to import (use barrel export index.ts)
- Testing: how to run tests, what's covered
acceptanceCriteria: README.md exists in src/lib/ml-design/
Lists all files with 1-line descriptions
Clearly marks which files are orphaned/unused
Import examples provided
Test running instructions included

title: Add JSDoc 'why' comments to neural-net.ts training loop
description:
neural-net.ts train() function (lines 129-218) implements backpropagation but has minimal inline comments. A developer reading this to understand the algorithm would struggle.

Add explanatory comments:
- Line 136: // Iterate over all training samples (online SGD — update after each sample)
- Line 149: // MSE loss: average squared difference between prediction and target
- Line 166: // Backpropagation: compute error signal (delta) for each layer in reverse
- Line 173: // Output delta = error × activation_derivative (chain rule at output)
- Line 175: // Clip gradient to [-5, 5] to prevent exploding gradients in deep networks
- Line 181: // Hidden layer delta: propagate error backward through weights
- Line 198: // SGD weight update: w -= learning_rate × delta × input (gradient descent step)
- Line 203: // Each weight changes proportionally to both the error signal and the input that caused it

This makes the code serve as a readable reference for students AND contributors.
acceptanceCriteria: Every non-obvious line in train() has an inline comment
Comments explain WHY (algorithm concept), not WHAT (code mechanics)
Comments reference algorithm concepts: chain rule, gradient descent, MSE
A mid-level developer can understand backpropagation by reading this file
No code changes — comments only

title: Create GitHub issue template for new ML Design concepts
description:
.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/new_algorithm.yml exists for the Algorithms module but not for ML Design. Create new_ml_concept.yml with:
- Concept name
- Category dropdown (Neural Network, CNN, Regularization, MLOps, Statistics, Experimentation)
- Description of what the concept teaches
- Prerequisites (what should the student know first)
- Real-world application example
- Suggested visualization approach
- Complexity/difficulty level
- References (papers, textbooks, courses)
acceptanceCriteria: new_ml_concept.yml exists in .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/
Category dropdown covers all 6+ ML concept categories
Required fields: name, category, description, prerequisites
Optional fields: visualization approach, references
Template encourages educational thinking (why does this concept matter?)

title: Create PR review checklist specific to ML Design contributions
description:
No review criteria exist for ML Design PRs. Create a PR template or reviewer guide:

**Engine Quality:**
- [ ] Implementation mathematically correct (verified against textbook/paper)
- [ ] Edge cases handled (empty input, extreme values)
- [ ] Zero `any` types
- [ ] JSDoc on all exported functions
- [ ] Unit tests written and passing

**Educational Quality:**
- [ ] Hook paragraph explains WHY this concept matters
- [ ] Intuition paragraph uses analogy (no jargon without explanation)
- [ ] Step descriptions say WHY not just WHAT
- [ ] Real-world application mentioned
- [ ] Key takeaways (3 bullets) provided

**Visual Quality:**
- [ ] Colors from CSS variables (no hardcoded hex)
- [ ] Legend present for all color-coded states
- [ ] Responsive (adapts to container size)
- [ ] Reduced-motion support

**Platform Integration:**
- [ ] Mode registered in exhaustive switches
- [ ] Cross-module bridge links added where relevant
- [ ] Concepts added to command palette search
acceptanceCriteria: PR checklist exists as .github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE/ml-design.md or as a doc
Covers engine, educational, visual, and platform integration categories
Each item is specific and testable (not vague)
Referenced in the contributor guide (MLD-185)

title: Add scoped test command: pnpm test:ml-design
description:
No scoped test command exists for ML Design. Running `pnpm test` runs ALL tests across all modules. For a contributor working on ML Design, add a scoped command:
```json
"test:ml-design": "vitest run src/lib/ml-design"
"test:ml-design:watch": "vitest src/lib/ml-design"
```
Also add a smoke test that imports MLDesignModule and verifies it renders without crashing.
acceptanceCriteria: pnpm test:ml-design runs only ML Design tests
pnpm test:ml-design:watch runs in watch mode
Smoke test verifies MLDesignModule.tsx renders all 6 modes without crash
Test output clearly shows which ML tests passed/failed

title: Add developer-facing error messages for common ML mode registration mistakes
acceptanceCriteria: Dev-mode console.error for modes missing from MODE_TABS
Error message includes file path and line number
Only runs in development (stripped in production)
Covers: MODE_TABS, sidebar rendering, canvas rendering, properties rendering
A new developer sees a clear error instead of silent failure

title: Document magic numbers with named constants and comments
description:
Several magic numbers in MLDesignModule.tsx lack explanation:
- Line 75: DATASET_SAMPLE_COUNT = 200 — named but no comment WHY 200
- Line 76: GRID_RESOLUTION = 30 — no comment WHY 30 (tradeoff: higher = smoother but slower)
- Line 1235: cellSize = 28 — why 28px? (fits 8×8 grid in ~224px)
- Line 2927: FS_SVG_W = 820 — why 820? (fits all nodes with spacing)
- Line 2928: FS_SVG_H = 420 — why 420?
- Line 1879: svgW = 700, svgH = 300 — ServingCanvas dimensions
- Line 2407: svgW = 660, svgH = 300 — ABTestingCanvas dimensions

Add comments explaining the reasoning behind each constant.
acceptanceCriteria: Every magic number constant has a comment explaining WHY that value
Comments reference the tradeoff (e.g., 'Higher resolution = smoother boundary but more forward passes')
Non-obvious pixel dimensions explain what they accommodate
No code changes — comments only
